label: State-space transitions

node.label: State-space transitions
node.definition: Latent state evolves according to learned transition and observation operators.
node.why_it_matters: The role of State-space transitions is to connect Differential equations, Dynamical systems & control theory to downstream capabilities including Selective state-space scan, Structured state-space models (S4 family), Selective state-space models (Mamba family).
examples: substrate for Structured state-space models (S4 family)
substrate for Selective state-space models (Mamba family)
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Differential equations, Dynamical systems & control theory.

dependencies.rationale: State-space transitions uses Differential equations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
State-space transitions uses Dynamical systems & control theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
State-space transitions uses Sequences, events & time series as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
State-space transitions uses Linear algebra as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
